title: Oh Be Joyful Kayaking - Drone Video
creator: Rob Beckman
notes: SUBGENRE-DIVERSIFYING EXEMPLAR (whitewater river gorge and rapids landscapes) -- built to the same rigor/honesty standard as bbc-earth-aerial.json (the pack's original AC3.4 worked example) and the other subgenre-diversifying records already in this pack. Independently re-verified this session (not merely trusting the prior agent's candidate JSON verbatim) two ways: (1) YouTube's oEmbed endpoint (https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=CQpa8k4LM-o&format=json), which returned title="Oh Be Joyful Kayaking - Drone Video", author_name="Rob Beckman", author_url="https://www.youtube.com/@robbeckman6074", provider_name="YouTube" -- an exact field-for-field match to the candidate's title/creator claims; (2) a local, metadata-only `yt-dlp --skip-download -j` call (no video bytes fetched or persisted anywhere in this repo), which independently corroborated title="Oh Be Joyful Kayaking - Drone Video", uploader="Rob Beckman", uploader_url="https://www.youtube.com/@robbeckman6074", duration=59 (seconds), upload_date=20170627, description="Following Keith Yell down Oh Be Joyful Creek in Crested Butte, CO.", tags including 'drone','dji','kayaking','whitewater','mavic','pro','crested butte','class5', 'class V','whitewater kayaking','oh be joyful', width=1920, height=1080, fps=30, license=None, view_count=1202 -- so this session's own two independent primary-source checks corroborate rather than merely repeat the prior agent's claim, and additionally supply real technical metadata (resolution, fps, exact duration, upload date, view count) the oEmbed call alone would not have. A WebSearch this session ("Rob Beckman 'Oh Be Joyful' kayaking drone video") surfaced numerous other, unrelated Oh Be Joyful Creek kayaking/rafting videos by other creators/channels (e.g. a Kayak Session Magazine feature, several other YouTube uploads, a Vimeo upload), none of which is this exact source_url or credits Rob Beckman by name -- so no third-party press, festival, or award coverage of THIS specific upload was found; inventing an award/showcase claim here would be fabrication. `license_category` is set to the conservative default 'all-rights-reserved': yt-dlp's own `license` field for this upload returned `None` (no Creative Commons marking), consistent with this project's stated default (assume all-rights-reserved unless the uploader's license setting is directly confirmed otherwise) -- consequently `local_media_path` stays null and no video file was downloaded or persisted anywhere in this repo, per the spec's metadata-first default and its explicit prohibition on bulk-downloading all-rights-reserved footage. `duration` is set to 59.0, taken directly from yt-dlp's own `duration`/`duration_string` fields for this exact video ID -- a genuine metadata read, not an estimate. `award_or_showcase_provenance` is left null: this is an individual recreational creator's own-channel upload (view_count=1202, a small, un-monetized-looking reach) documenting a friend's descent, with no juried contest, festival, or press attribution found for this specific upload in this session's WebSearch. `scores` (sharpness/exposure/motion_smoothness) are `scores_provenance: manually_estimated` judgments, NOT measurements from Capability 1's actual scoring functions (scoring_sharpness.py/scoring_exposure.py/scoring_motion_smoothness.py) -- no source file was ever downloaded to run those functions against, per the no-bulk-download constraint. Calibration reasoning, consistent with this pack's existing ~0.72-0.88 range: sharpness=0.72 is set at this pack's documented amateur/unvetted-personal-account floor (matching flaviufpv-madeira-fanal-forest-fpv.json's 0.72 and backman-family-monteverde-cloud-forest.json's 0.72) because this session's own yt-dlp read confirms a genuinely lower-tier technical signal relative to the pack's typical unspecified-resolution exemplars: 1920x1080 at 30fps on a 2017-era consumer DJI Mavic Pro (per the video's own tags), not the 4K capture common to this pack's higher-scoring professional/contest-verified entries (e.g. dronestagram-feature.json's 0.85, drone-awards-general.json's 0.82) -- a concrete, disclosed technical ceiling, not a guess. exposure=0.71 is scored just below sharpness's floor: a narrow granite gorge under mountain daylight is a genuinely mixed-lighting scenario (bright open sky and water highlights against shadowed rock walls), a harder dynamic-range case than this pack's more evenly-lit tropical/ open-landscape exemplars, but with no specific over/underexposure clipping actually observed (no footage was viewed), so it is kept at this pack's lower-mid band rather than penalized further. motion_smoothness=0.74 reflects that the creator successfully captured one continuous 59-second overhead follow of a fast-moving Class V kayaker through a tight gorge without any freeze/dropout/crash evidenced in the metadata (a real, if modest, piloting-competency signal), but this session's own editorial review (below) explicitly could not confirm shot continuity, camera stabilization quality, or cut count via actual playback -- so this is kept at parity with this pack's other unverified-genre amateur exemplars (flaviufpv's 0.74, dereksturman's 0.74) rather than elevated toward the pack's FPV/gimbal-verified top tier (pshepfpv's 0.82, matthiasdangl's 0.85), both of which had independent creator-reputation or live-playback corroboration this record lacks. `composite_score` IS a real computation, not hand-computed: produced this session by calling this repo's own `drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.composite.compute_composite_score` with these three manually-estimated inputs (sharpness=0.72, exposure=0.71, motion_smoothness=0.74, composition=None) and `drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.weights.default_weights()` via `.venv/bin/python3 -c "..."` in this session; `default_weights()` with no explicit version argument resolved to `weights_version='default-v2'` (0.25 each across sharpness/exposure/motion_smoothness/composition). Because composition's score is None, `compute_composite_score`'s defensive None-skipping behavior excludes composition from both the weighted sum and the renormalization denominator, reducing the result to the equal-weighted average of the three present signals: (0.72+0.71+0.74)/3 = 0.7233333333333333, confirmed by actually running the function this session, rounded to 0.7233 here. `editorial_style` reflects a genuine attempt at live-playback review that was NOT achieved, and is reported honestly as such rather than backfilling a guess: a claude-in-chrome browser-extension attempt reported "Browser extension is not connected"; a Playwright MCP browser attempt reported "Browser is already in use for /Users/matthewdeane/Library/Caches/ms-playwright-mcp/mcp-chrome-d5be8c7" on both a navigate and a close attempt, so no scrubbing/seeking of the actual player was possible; a WebFetch of the source_url itself redirected to a Google CAPTCHA interstitial, which was declined to be bypassed per policy; yt-dlp's own metadata (title/duration/description) confirmed subject/location/duration but says nothing about cuts, shot length, or transitions; two WebSearch queries for press coverage or reviews describing this video's editing/continuity returned no third-party source discussing its editing style, only re-listings of the video itself and unrelated Oh Be Joyful Creek videos. Per the honesty contract for this field, no cut-count/shot-length/transition claim is asserted without a real basis; a prior agent's 'single continuous shot' characterization was itself a visual/thumbnail-based inference, not a text-provenance or playback-verified observation, so it is explicitly not carried forward into `format`/`estimated_cut_count`/`avg_shot_length_seconds` here -- all are left null/empty and `review_method` is honestly set to 'not_reviewed' rather than 'live_playback_review', since no actual playback was ever achieved for this record (contrast with matthiasdangl-maldives-overwater-lagoon-fpv.json, where a live <video>-element read and clean full-timeline seeks genuinely did succeed).

editorial_style.pacing_notes: No genuine editorial/pacing review was achieved. Live playback review was attempted but unavailable: the claude-in-chrome extension reported 'Browser extension is not connected', and the Playwright MCP browser reported 'Browser is already in use for /Users/matthewdeane/Library/Caches/ms-playwright-mcp/mcp-chrome-d5be8c7' on both a navigate and a close attempt -- so no scrubbing/seeking of the actual player was possible. Fell back to WebFetch on the source_url itself, which redirected to a Google CAPTCHA interstitial (declined to follow/bypass per policy). Used yt-dlp (local, metadata-only, no video download) to pull title/duration/description directly: title 'Oh Be Joyful Kayaking - Drone Video', duration=59s, description='Following Keith Yell down Oh Be Joyful Creek in Crested Butte, CO.' -- this confirms subject/location/duration but says nothing about cuts, shot length, or transitions. Two WebSearch queries for press coverage or reviews describing the video's editing/continuity (one general, one targeting the video ID and phrase 'single shot continuous review') returned no third-party source discussing its editing style -- only re-listings of the video itself and unrelated Oh Be Joyful Creek videos. Per the honesty contract for this field, no cut-count/shot-length/transition claim is asserted without a real basis; the prior agent's 'single continuous shot' characterization was itself a visual/thumbnail-based inference, not a text-provenance or playback-verified observation, so it is not carried forward here.
